source.name: gstack by Garry Tan — GStack Browser QA flow

goal: Define a browser QA workflow using persistent Chromium sessions for testing web applications — form testing, login-state verification, screenshot-based regression, and automated bug-fix loops without cold-start overhead.
when_to_use: Multi-step user journey testing with login state
inputs_required: Running browser daemon with persistent session
steps: Load target URL in a tab
For each test scenario: execute actions, screenshot key states, check expected results
On failure: atomic commit fix, re-run regression test
Generate QA report: pass/fail/skip with evidence screenshots
Classify issues as P0 (blocker), P1 (important), P2 (cosmetic)
commands_or_apis: snapshot — interactive page snapshot with natural language element identification
type — enter text into form fields
verification: P0 issues resolved before Ship gate
failure_modes: Element selectors break after UI change (use multiple fallback selectors)
Timing issues with async content loading (use wait-for patterns)
Cookie/session expires during long QA session
CAPTCHA appears during automated flow (use handoff mechanism)
safety_notes: Do not run QA against production with real user data
Do not screenshot pages containing PII/passwords
Use test accounts, not real user accounts
QA browser environment should be isolated from personal browsing

quality.notes: QA workflow pattern extracted from gstack's /qa and /qa-only skills. The persistent session approach eliminates the 3-5s cold-start penalty per command and enables login-state-dependent testing.

retrieval.ai_summary: Browser QA workflow with persistent Chromium sessions for web application testing. Execute test scenarios with screenshots, classify issues as P0/P1/P2, auto-fix bugs atomically with regression re-testing, handle CAPTCHA via handoff mechanism, and generate structured QA reports.
